Question: Should patients with MTX-naïve and non-MTX csDMARDs exposed RA and moderate to high disease activity receive MTX monotherapy or boDMARD monotherapy or tsDMARD monotherapy?
P: Adults with rheumatoid arthritis who are methotrexate (MTX)-naïve and have not been previously exposed to conventional synthetic DMARDs (csDMARDs), with moderate to high disease activity (e.g., DAS28-CRP ≥3.2 or CDAI >10)
C: Biologic DMARD (boDMARD) monotherapy or targeted synthetic DMARD (tsDMARD) monotherapy

Biologic DMARD (boDMARD) monotherapy or: Achievement of low disease activity or remission at 6 months, safety (e.g., serious adverse events, treatment discontinuation due to adverse events), and radiographic progression (e.g., change in Sharp/van der Heijde score at 12 months)
